data.title: Gungnir Resources CEO resigns
data.description:
• Gungnir Resources (TSXV:GUG:CA) announced on Thursday the  resignation of Jari Paakki as Chief Executive Officer and a director of the company effective September 30, 2025. 
  • Paakki advised the company that his resignation is for personal reasons. 
  • The board will be undertaking a search for a new CEO.

data.title: Alaska Air to convert part of its Boeing 787 order to larger 787-10's
Smithfield Foods announces pricing of upsized secondary offering
data.description:
• Smithfield Foods (NASDAQ:SFD) announced on Thursday the pricing of a secondary public offering of 19.53M shares of common stock of the company sold by SFDS UK Holdings, the principal shareholder of the company, at a price to the public of $23.25 per share. 
  • In addition, the selling shareholder has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.93M additional shares of the company’s common stock at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions. 
  • The company is not selling any shares of common stock in the offering and will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the shares by the selling shareholder.

data.title: Japan's real wages turn positive for first time in eight months to 0.5% in July, nominal wage growth hits 7-month high
data.description:
Japan's nominal wages jumped 4.1% year-over-year in July, beating expectations and marking the fastest growth in seven months. After months of decline, real wages—a measure of purchasing power—grew by 0.5%, the first positive reading since last December.

The positive shift was a result of consistent base pay increases and large summer bonuses.

Separately, the United States will cut tariffs on Japanese automobile imports to 15% by the end of the month, following an executive order signed by President Donald Trump on Thursday, Reuters reported. 

Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba reaffirmed his intention to remain in office after the U.S. finalized a trade deal lowering auto tariffs, rejecting speculation that the agreement might prompt his resignation.

Japan’s foreign reserves climbed by $19.8 billion to $1.32 trillion in August 2025, the highest since July 2022. 

The Nikkei 225 Index jumped 1.2% to around 43,100 and the Topix gained 0.8% to 3,105 on Friday, with Japanese equities rising for a second session. The Japanese yen strengthened toward 148 per dollar on Friday, recouping losses from the previous session, after US President Donald Trump signed an executive order cutting tariffs on Japanese cars to 15% from 27.5%. 

More on Japan

  • BoJ minutes reveal ongoing caution on economy amid inflation & market risks
  • Japan's manufacturing PMI revised upward to 48.7 in April
  • Japan to spend ¥388 billion on relief measures for U.S. tariff impact
  • Japan producer prices rise more than expected to 4.2% in March
  • Japan’s unemployment rate unexpectedly drops to 2.4% in February

data.title: OpenAI to launch its first AI chip with Broadcom: report
data.description:
OpenAI is  set to produce its first artificial intelligence chip next year in partnership with U.S. semiconductor company Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

According to the report, OpenAI plans to put the chip to use internally rather than make it available to external customers. 

OpenAI and Broadcom did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment on the report. 

OpenAI, which has successfully commercialized generative AI capabilities, relies on substantial computing power to train and run its systems.

There were reports earlier this year that OpenAI is pushing ahead on its plan to reduce its reliance on Nvidia for its chip supply by developing its first generation of in-house AI silicon.

OpenAI's move follows efforts by other tech giants such as Google (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) and Meta (META), which have built custom chips to handle AI workloads.

data.title: Trump finalizes Japan trade deal with 15% tariffs
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Thursday to put into effect a trade deal with Japan, with 15% baseline tariffs on most Japanese goods, including autos. 

As part of the newly signed trade agreement, Japan has agreed to invest $550B in projects selected by the U.S. government. 

Tokyo has also committed to increasing its purchase of American agricultural products such as corn and soybeans, along with U.S.-made commercial aircraft and defense equipment. 

According to the executive order, U.S. will apply a baseline tariff of 15% on all Japanese imports, with sector-specific levies for automobiles, aerospace products, and pharmaceutical goods. 

The order also prevents Trump’s country-specific tariffs on top of existing levies. 

Japan has agreed to offer the U.S. “breakthrough openings in market access” in the manufacturing, aerospace, agriculture, and automobile sectors. 

The Japanese yen (JPY:USD) was last down 0.88% against the U.S. dollar. 

Japan-based ETFs to track: (NYSEARCA:EWJ) (DXJ) (DFJ) (HEWJ) (DBJP) (FLJP) (FXY) (YCL) (YCS)
